_comment: Pre-measured bone data for the default animated character archetypes. Used by the asset design pipeline to size furniture to character scale. Re-measure after .anim file changes.

humanoid_normal.method: get_bone_positions in standing-idle pose, world Y minus floor Y=16

seating._seat_top_note: Popliteal height — feet flat on floor when seated. Subcubes cannot hit this exactly.
seating._achievable_note: sy=1 in cube Y=0 (top at 0.667). 0.188 above ideal; legs slope slightly downward — acceptable.
seating.seat_subcube_recipe: sy=1 in cube cy=0 (S cx 0 cz sx 1 sz Wood)
seating._leg_note: sy=0 in cube cy=0 fills exactly floor-to-seat-bottom (Y=0 to Y=0.333).
seating._depth_note: Thigh length = hip_bottom - popliteal. Seat must be at least this deep.
seating._depth_subcubes_note: 2 subcubes = 0.667 cubes; accommodates full thigh with slight overhang.
seating._width_note: 2 subcubes = 0.667 cubes; ~10% wider than estimated hip width. Minimum 1 subcube (0.333).
seating._backrest_note: Lumbar at ~0.868 must be covered. Chair backs stop at ~1.667 (shoulder blade). Thrones go to 2.0.
seating._facing_note: yaw=0 is empirically correct for humanoid_normal sitting. At yaw=0 the sitting animation's legs extend in +Z (forward, away from backrest at sz=0). At yaw=pi legs go through the backrest. Backrest placed at sz=0 (-Z side).

tables._standing_note: Elbow height when standing is ~1.1; table at 1.0 cube is comfortable.
tables._seated_note: Seated elbow height; leave clearance above seat top (0.667) of at least 0.200.
